title: Optimization of Large Language Models with Multimodal Data Training for Fault Diagnosis in Wind Turbine Systems

agents.description: A monolithic hybrid CNN-LLM agent that processes multimodal sensor data (vibration, acoustic, thermal, visual) through a CNN for feature extraction and fault classification, then uses an LLM to generate human-readable diagnostic reports with fault type identification and corrective action recommendations.

genai_model.finetuning_dataset_description: The LLM is trained/fine-tuned using multimodal fault diagnosis data collected from 36 operational 2.5MW wind turbines, comprising 12,480 synchronized samples across three fault categories: bearing failure, gearbox malfunction, and blade damage.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: The system follows a single-step pipeline: multimodal sensor data is collected and preprocessed, features are extracted via WPT and MFCCs, fused through cross-modal attention gates into a unified feature vector, classified by the CNN, and then passed to the LLM to generate a diagnostic report. The flow is sequential and automated without iterative agent loops.

human_integration.human_integration_description: The system generates human-readable diagnostic reports for wind turbine technicians, providing fault type identification, severity assessments, and actionable maintenance recommendations. Human technicians (n=12) evaluated report clarity and actionability. The system is designed to assist operators in fault resolution through an AI dialogue interface, improving decision-making support.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Predictive maintenance and fault diagnosis for wind turbine systems, targeting mechanical, electrical, and control system failures to reduce maintenance costs and downtime.
retrieval.problem_description: Wind turbines are subject to diverse faults (bearing failure, gearbox malfunction, blade damage) that lead to costly maintenance and downtime. Traditional single-modality diagnostic methods relying on predefined thresholds are limited in capturing the full range of fault types, are vulnerable to noise and varying conditions, and lack interpretability for technicians.
retrieval.proposed_solution: An integrated multimodal fault diagnosis system combining a hybrid CNN-LLM architecture. The CNN processes fused vibration, acoustic, thermal, and visual sensor data using wavelet packet transform and MFCCs for feature extraction, cross-modal attention for fusion, and supervised classification. The LLM converts the diagnostic output into human-readable reports with fault identification and corrective action recommendations, deployed in real-time via SCADA integration.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: The study conducts controlled experiments on a dataset from 36 operational wind turbines (12,480 samples), comparing the proposed CNN-LLM hybrid against SVM and DNN baselines using accuracy, F1-score, AUC-ROC, BLEU, ROUGE-L, and METEOR metrics. Ablation studies quantify component contributions. Field deployment over three months validates real-world operational impact.

decision_quality_improvement: CNN-LLM hybrid achieved 91.5% overall fault classification accuracy, outperforming SVM (84.8%) by 6.7% and DNN (88.3%) by 3.2%
AUC-ROC of 0.96 vs SVM 0.88 and DNN 0.91
Cross-modal attention improved gearbox fault detection F1-score by 5.9%
LLM integration enhanced blade damage recall by 2.3%
LLM-generated reports achieved BLEU-4 of 0.84, ROUGE-L of 0.76, METEOR of 0.60
Human evaluation: report clarity 4.2/5.0 and actionability 4.4/5.0, vs template-based 2.8/5.0
Outperforms GE Predix (86.2%) and Siemens MindSphere (88.7%) in accuracy
response_time_impact: Mean fault detection latency of 8.7 minutes
Mean inference latency of 320ms (exceeds 250ms industry real-time threshold for pitch control)
4.5 hours/week downtime reduction compared to SCADA-only systems
GE Predix latency 520ms, Siemens MindSphere 480ms, proposed 320ms
reliability_metrics: 93.2% true positive rate at 5% false alarm rate for bearing faults
23.4% reduction in maintenance costs over three-month field deployment
56% fewer emergency repairs over three-month field deployment
<5ms inter-sensor alignment error achieved via SCADA timestamps and DTW

ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: LLM fine-tuned on real-world wind turbine fault data achieved BLEU-4 of 0.84 and high technician actionability ratings (4.4/5.0)

failure_modes: Performance degradation under heavy rain: blade F1-score drops to 0.78
320ms mean latency exceeds 250ms real-time threshold for pitch control in high-speed wind conditions
Insufficient labeled data for rare or compound faults
limitations: Mean inference latency of 320ms exceeds 250ms industry real-time threshold for pitch control
Performance degrades under extreme weather conditions, particularly heavy rain
Reliance on high-quality labeled data; obtaining sufficient fault samples for rare or compound faults is difficult
Weather sensitivity affecting visual and acoustic processing
Deployment maturity trails commercial alternatives (GE Predix, Siemens MindSphere)

identified_research_gaps: Reducing inference latency below 250ms threshold for real-time pitch control via neuromorphic computing or lightweight model architectures
Improving robustness under extreme weather conditions (e.g., heavy rain) for visual and acoustic processing
Addressing reliance on high-quality labeled data, especially for rare or compound faults, through physics-informed data augmentation, federated learning, and self-supervised learning
Edge deployment optimization through model compression and quantization
Extension of the framework to other renewable energy systems (solar farms, hydroelectric plants)
Integration of digital twin technology for scaling predictive maintenance
Advancements in LLM interpretability and multimodal fusion techniques for human-machine collaboration
